headline: 1 Services Stock with Promising Prospects and 2 Facing Headwinds
summary: Business services providers thrive by solving complex operational challenges for their clients, allowing them to focus on their secret sauce. Furthermore, the demand for their offerings is rising as more clients outsource non-core functions, a trend that has enabled the industry to return 14.3% over the past six months. At the same time, the S&P 500 was up 9.8%.

At the same time, the S&P 500 was up 9.8%.

headline: IBM to invest $10 billion for large-scale quantum computer by 2029
summary: IBM said on Thursday it plans to invest more than $10 billion in quantum computing over five years as it ‌aims to build by 2029 the first large-scale quantum computer capable ‌of running complex calculations reliably and without errors.  The announcement follows a Trump administration decision last week ​to take $2 billion in equity stakes in nine quantum computing companies, with IBM set to receive half of the funding for a new venture called Anderon that would become the first dedicated quantum chip manufacturing facility in the U.S.  The move, ‌part of the administration's push ⁠to secure U.S. leadership in the emerging technology and counter China, underscores the growing prominence of quantum computing.

content.title: IBM to invest $10 billion for large-scale quantum computer by 2029
content.summary: IBM said on Thursday it plans to invest more than $10 billion in quantum computing over five years as it ‌aims to build by 2029 the first large-scale quantum computer capable ‌of running complex calculations reliably and without errors.  The announcement follows a Trump administration decision last week ​to take $2 billion in equity stakes in nine quantum computing companies, with IBM set to receive half of the funding for a new venture called Anderon that would become the first dedicated quantum chip manufacturing facility in the U.S.  The move, ‌part of the administration's push ⁠to secure U.S. leadership in the emerging technology and counter China, underscores the growing prominence of quantum computing.

headline: IBM commits $5 billion to secure open-source software
summary: IBM said on Thursday it has committed $5 billion to an initiative that will deploy engineers and AI tools ‌to help companies better secure open source software.  The initiative, called ‌Project Lightwell, seeks to create a "clearinghouse" for open source security, establishing a model for managing ​risks across the software supply chain.  Open source software is freely available code that anyone can use and modify, and powers the technology systems of most companies.

content.title: IBM commits $5 billion to secure open-source software
content.summary: IBM said on Thursday it has committed $5 billion to an initiative that will deploy engineers and AI tools ‌to help companies better secure open source software.  The initiative, called ‌Project Lightwell, seeks to create a "clearinghouse" for open source security, establishing a model for managing ​risks across the software supply chain.  Open source software is freely available code that anyone can use and modify, and powers the technology systems of most companies.

headline: Exclusive: IBM launches $5 billion AI push to combat cyber threats

headline: IBM and Red Hat Commit $5 Billion to Redefine the Future of Open Source in the AI Era
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) and Red Hat today announced Project Lightwell, a $5 billion commitment backed by new frontier AI capabilities and a global force of more than 20,000 engineers to help enterprises secure open source software. Together, these investments establish a new model for enterprise use of open source software, from upstream development through production environments.

content.title: IBM and Red Hat Commit $5 Billion to Redefine the Future of Open Source in the AI Era
content.summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) and Red Hat today announced Project Lightwell, a $5 billion commitment backed by new frontier AI capabilities and a global force of more than 20,000 engineers to help enterprises secure open source software. Together, these investments establish a new model for enterprise use of open source software, from upstream development through production environments.

headline: Hongli Group Inc. Engages Battery Technology Expert Dr. Phillip A. Medina IV as Independent Technical Advisor for Energy Storage Initiatives
summary: Hongli Group Inc. (Nasdaq: HLP) (the "Company" or "Hongli Group"), a cold roll formed steel profile manufacturer, today announced that it has engaged Dr. Phillip A. Medina IV as an independent technical advisor to provide consulting and advisory support in connection with the Company's evaluation of opportunities in the new energy technology, advanced material and energy storage sectors.

headline: IBM Stock Gains As Tech Giant Pledges $10 Billion In Quantum Investment
summary: IBM stock climbed Thursday as the tech giant pledged to invest more than $10 billion over five years in its quantum computing efforts.  The news comes a week after a $1 billion award from the federal government for quantum computing gave IBM shares a major jolt.  The $10 billion toward includes research and development funds, capital expenditures and mergers and acquisitions, IBM said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

headline: Lightedge Expands IBM Hybrid Cloud Portfolio with Support for IBM Power Virtual Server
summary: Lightedge, operator of the nation's largest private IBM Power Cloud by number of LPARs, today announced the integration of IBM Power Virtual Server (PowerVS) into its core hybrid cloud offering. The expansion further solidifies Lightedge's position as the definitive partner for enterprises running mission-critical IBM Power workloads, giving IBM i and AIX customers a seamless path to any infrastructure destination — private, hybrid, or public cloud.

Project Lightwell builds on IBM’s partnerships with major financial institutions and recent US federal quantum funding activity. For you as an investor, Project Lightwell reinforces IBM’s position as a core infrastructure and security partner for...

headline: Tokenmaxxing is over. That’s because it never measured what really counts to see ROI from AI
summary: Token usage is a poor proxy for firm-wide productivity gains. Those only come with workflow redesign.

content.title: Tokenmaxxing is over. That’s because it never measured what really counts to see ROI from AI
content.summary: Token usage is a poor proxy for firm-wide productivity gains. Those only come with workflow redesign.

thumbnail.caption: As recently as a few weeks ago, a number of tech firms encouraged 'tokenmaxxing,'  incentivizing employees to spend as many AI tokens (the basic data units by which AI usage is metered) as possible. It was seen as a proxy for using AI agents to boost productivity. Those 'tokenmaxxing' days are over.
